summary: Backend wallet creation fix in progress. Sub-tasks 1-6 complete (createWallet function, secure randomness, BIP-39 mnemonic, secp256k1 key derivation, EVM address computation, wallet storage with 9-address limit). Remaining: return full credentials once, secure listWallets, and improve error handling.
architectural_notes: Wallet storage enforces a max of 9 addresses per user principal; only the address is stored (no private keys or seed phrases).
createWallet uses Random.blob() for entropy, BIP-39 mnemonic generation, and secp256k1 key derivation to produce EVM 0x addresses.

existing_features.title: Update error handling in `createWallet` so that all failure cases return a structured, human-readable error variant instead of propagating raw trap messages. The frontend's `walletErrors.ts` utility should be able to map every returned error variant to a clean user-facing string, eliminating the generic 'Failed to create wallet' message.

new_feature_requests.summary: Return full wallet credentials (address, privateKey, recoveryPhrase, chain) exactly once from createWallet
Ensure listWallets does not return private keys or seed phrases
Update error handling so the frontend receives a clean result instead of 'Failed to create wallet'

In the `createWallet` backend function, ensure the return value includes the full wallet credentials — `address`, `privateKey`, `recoveryPhrase`, and `chain` — exactly once upon successful creation. The private key and recovery phrase must only be present in this single response and must never be persisted in stable storage.
Ensure the `listWallets` backend function returns only wallet addresses (and any non-sensitive metadata such as `chain`). It must never return `privateKey` or `recoveryPhrase` fields.
